exemplar.title: How does Liza Donnelly use humor and satire in her cartoons to challenge societal attitudes toward sexual violence, and to what extent does her work empower or critique the victims and perpetrators in this context?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Liza Donnelly use humor and satire in her cartoons to challenge societal attitudes toward sexual violence, and to what extent does her work empower or critique the victims and perpetrators in this context?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Demonstrates solid factual knowledge of Donnellyâs career and feminist waves
Sustains interpretation of implications related to the guiding question across multiple paragraphs
Effectively uses contextual details (#MeToo, internalised misogyny, toxic masculinity) to frame each cartoon
weaknesses: Discussion sometimes drifts into broad social commentary not tightly tied to specific images
References are inconsistent in formatting and occasional claims lack page-specific evidence
Some citations are incomplete or mis-formatted, reducing reliability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good knowledge and understanding with sustained interpretation of implications in relation to the topic (Band 4). While contextual background is detailed and interpretations are maintained, the inconsistent and incomplete referencing prevents the analysis from reaching the highest band.

strengths: Identifies and explains visual features such as posture, expression and juxtaposition in several cartoons
Makes some insightful connections to secondary concepts (e.g., Lakoffâs low-modality adverbs)
Labels devices like irony and satire to underpin evaluative points
weaknesses: Explanations often stop at identifying devices rather than exploring how they shape reader response
Evaluation is intermittent, with several paragraphs relying on description or moral commentary
Lacks consistently convincing or in-depth analysis of authorial choices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay moves beyond description at times (identifies irony, links to modality), much of the evaluation is superficial and reliant on descriptive commentary. Insight is uneven, aligning more closely with Band 2 than the sustained, generally appropriate analysis of Band 3.

strengths: Clear overall structure with defined introduction, body sections for each cartoon, and conclusion
Topic sentences and section headers signal a coherent line of inquiry into how satire challenges attitudes
Supporting examples are usually integrated into analysis rather than simply appended
weaknesses: Some paragraphs are loosely linked or repeat similar points, weakening cohesion
Abrupt transitions (e.g., sentence fragments, isolated footnotes) disrupt flow
Integration of captions and footnotes sometimes interrupts reader engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization is adequate with a discernible line of inquiry and topic development. Examples are integrated sometimes, but occasional lapses in cohesion and flow keep this at Band 3.

strengths: Language is clear and readable with varied sentence structures
Appropriate use of academic vocabulary (e.g., âjuxtaposition,â âbinary oppositionâ)
Register remains mostly formal and analytical throughout
weaknesses: Minor grammatical errors, inconsistent capitalization and occasional run-on sentences
Word-processing artefacts (stray line breaks, typos) appear
Bibliographic entries contain misspellings and style inconsistencies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear and adequate accuracy in grammar and vocabulary, with an academic register maintained. Occasional lapses and errors prevent it from reaching the precise, high-accuracy expected in Band 4, placing it solidly in Band 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting examples (cartoon images, quoted phrases such as âhair too provocativeâ) are usually placed within sentences and integrated grammatically. Nonetheless, several citations appear as footnote strings or parentheses after the paragraph rather than being woven into analysis, and the student occasionally summarises a whole cartoon without pinpointing particular frames or wording. This meets the descriptor for **âsometimes integratedâ** but not yet âmostly well integrated,â warranting Band 3.

The student demonstrates **solid factual knowledge** about Donnellyâs career, feminist waves and the chronology of individual cartoons. Contextual details (e.g. #MeToo, internalised misogyny, toxic masculinity) are used to frame each visual text, and commentary consistently links back to the guiding question of challenging societal attitudes toward sexual violence. Interpretations â such as reading the checklist as evidence of victim-blaming or seeing the âgood girl/slutâ dichotomy as media-imposed â are **sustained across several paragraphs**, showing more than a merely satisfactory grasp. However, discussion sometimes drifts into broad social commentary that is not tied tightly to the specific images, preventing the analysis from becoming fully persuasive. Hence the work fits âgoodâ¦ with sustained interpretationâ rather than the highest, âexcellent/persuasive,â band.

The essay follows a clear structure: introduction, separate sections for four cartoons, and a concise conclusion that returns to the research question. Topic sentences signal direction, and there is a discernible **line of inquiry** â how humour/satire challenges attitudes. Some paragraphs, however, are loosely linked or repeat similar points (victim-blaming, double standards), indicating only **adequate** rather than cohesive development. Transitions and synthesis between examples could be stronger, so Band 3 is appropriate.

References to the cartoons are **generally relevant**: figure numbers are provided, and captions or paraphrased dialogue are cited to support points about language, posture and composition. Scholarly or journalistic secondary sources are listed and occasionally quoted (e.g. Lakoff on modality). Nevertheless, quotations from the primary cartoons are limited to short lexical items; many claims (e.g. class/race differences, Donnellyâs personal assault) are asserted without page-specific evidence. Some citations are incomplete or mis-formatted, reducing reliability. Overall the textual support mostly backs the argument but lacks the consistency and finesse required for Band 4.

The student maintains an academic, analytical **register** for most of the essay, using third-person and discipline-specific terminology. Occasional informal phrasing (âthis is because of a double standardâ) or slight tonal drift into advocacy does not undermine the overall appropriateness. Stylistic choices are therefore **mostly appropriate** but not consistently polished or nuanced, placing the work in Band 3.

Overall expression is **clear and readable**; sentences are varied and vocabulary (e.g. âjuxtaposition,â âbinary oppositionâ) is appropriate. Minor errorsâmissing apostrophes, inconsistent capitalisation, word-processing artefacts (âperforon,â stray line breaks)âoccur but rarely obscure meaning. Thus accuracy is adequate rather than exemplary, aligning with Band 3.

The student moves beyond description by analysing visual placement, posture, interrogative mood, capitalisation and juxtaposition, which meets the criterion for **âgenerally appropriate analysis.â** Evaluation appears when the essay labels certain techniques ironic, exaggerated or satirical. Yet explanation often stops at identifying a device rather than exploring *how* it shapes reader response; several paragraphs slide into moral commentary rather than textual scrutiny. Because insight is intermittent rather than âat times insightfulâ or âconsistently convincing,â Band 3 is most accurate.

annotations.comment: The research question is articulated clearly but is split across separate lines, which can disrupt reader focus. Consider integrating it into a single coherent sentence to enhance clarity and flow.
The clear title placement and section header âHigher Level Essayâ effectively signal organizational structure, providing a strong framework for the reader.
This opening paragraph provides comprehensive background on Donnellyâs career and feminist waves, demonstrating solid contextual understanding.
The claim about Donnellyâs focus on misogyny lacks direct linkage to specific visual elements. Tie this assertion explicitly to parts of Figure 1 to strengthen the analysis.
The footnotes display inconsistent formatting (mixed <sup> tags, URLs, ibid). Implement a uniform citation style to enhance reliability and reader confidence.
The analysis of posture, expression and placement in Figure 1 perceptively shows how Donnelly visually conveys vulnerability and internalised doubt.
The sentence âShe has embodied the ideasâ¦â uses passive construction and ambiguous pronouns. Rephrase to clarify the subject and active meaning.
Use of âibidâ without context can confuse. Provide full citation details on the first occurrence to improve clarity.
This paragraph reiterates the concept of victim-blaming but only restates earlier observations. Deepen the evaluation by explaining how the ironic title shapes viewer judgment.
The linkage to Lakoffâs concept of low modality through the adverb âtooâ is insightful and effectively ties linguistic features to power dynamics.
When noting the lack of capitalization, cite the exact speech-bubble text style (lowercase vs uppercase) to substantiate your claim.
Your interpretation of intersecting class and race in Figure 2 is compelling but remains general. Specify which figures illustrate these differences (dress, setting, dialogue).
The sentence âDrinking is permissible to men but dangerous for women, this is because of a double standard.â is a run-on. Split or restructure for grammatical accuracy.
The opening âcompromising.â fragment creates an abrupt transition. Rephrase this sentence to maintain narrative flow and coherence.
Inclusion of the handwritten âHow to Testify as a Womanâ list enriches the analysis by engaging with Donnellyâs original satirical format.
The caption for Figure 4 is visually separated from its analysis. Integrate caption contextually within the paragraph for smoother reading.
The serious allegation of assault by a Supreme Court member is important but currently unsupported. Provide a credible source to substantiate this claim.
The enumeration of checklist items in Text/0 demonstrates detailed reading of satirical structure and effectively supports your interpretation.
The list of repeated âibidâ footnotes at the end interrupts the flow of the conclusion. Consolidate or reposition these references for coherence.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes how Donnellyâs satire provokes cultural reflection and calls to action, reinforcing your thesis.
Works Cited entries mix different citation styles (e.g., MLA book vs. web entry). Select and apply a single style guide consistently across all sources.
The duplicate EncyclopÃ¦dia Britannica entries appear redundant. Remove or merge repeats to streamline and avoid confusion.
The author name is misspelled as âJabocsâ instead of âJacobsâ in the bibliography. Correct this for precision and professionalism.
Breaking the URL into a separate line in Text/1 reduces readability. Consider embedding hyperlinks parenthetically or using a consistent inline format.
The citation for Ayhanâs dissertation mixes text and URL in an unstructured way. Format it to match your chosen style guideâs requirements for consistency.
